author: @somerandominternetdweller
text:
Company: Why won’t anyone buy our product?

Customer: Tells company what they wanted.

Company: Ignores customers feedback, criticizes customers, talk down and talk trash to customers. Also says: If you don’t like. Don’t buy it.

Customers: Flame company for being stupid and about their failure.

Company: meltdowns down*, damage control and deploys gaming “journalists”.

Customer: continues to flame.

Company: Mass layoffs.

Rinse, repeat.

author: @porchpirateofpestulon
text:
As a fan of the classic Marathon trilogy on my MAC back in the day (the classics are free on Steam btw), I love hearing their 'morale' is in 'free-fall'.

You want to revive things people love to desecrate them. Do you think fans of Marathon wanted an extraction shooter? Do you think this artwork is what fans wanted?

As a classic 90s series that was mostly confined to MAC, I'm betting many of you aren't familiar... so look up the artwork of Craig Mullins. He did the chapter artwork for the classic trilogy. I imagined a future where his artwork would be brought to life in potential sequels. Not the Jaguar commercial being turned into a video game.

author: @ginozeus2579
text:
For those of you who are wondering WHO might be running Bungie and Marathon into the ground in flames:
The Bungie Marathon team's leadership has undergone several changes. The current game director is Joe Ziegler, who took over from Christopher Barrett. Scott Taylor is the executive producer. Steve Cotton is the creative director. Other key roles include Jessica Irwin (Senior Producer) and Lya Vera (Designer). 
Here's a more detailed list of Bungie's current and former senior leadership:
Current Leadership:
Pete Parsons: CEO/President.
Danielle Porter: CFO.
Jonny Ebbert: Chief Creative Officer.
Ondraus Jenkins: Chief Strategy Officer.
Patrick O'Kelley: Chief Operating Officer.
Amy Johnson: Chief People Officer. 
Recent Departures:
Holly Barbacovi: Formerly Chief Operating/People Officer, now at Hasbro.
Luis Villegas: Formerly Chief Technology Officer, now at PlayStation.
Luke Smith and Mark Noseworthy: Destiny veterans who left Bungie.
Justin Truman: Chief Development Officer.
Jason Jones: Chief Vision Officer.
Don McGowan: Chief Legal Officer.
Holly Barbacovi: Chief People Officer. 
The current Franchise Art Director for Bungie's Marathon is Joseph Cross. He has been in this role since 2019 and is based in Seattle. Cross previously worked at Bungie as a Lead Concept Artist. 
Bungie is full of woke thieving ass pretend devs that love pronouns and Ai tools. Prove me wrong🤨

author: @lawlietriver8869
text: They could entirely recover from all of these mistakes and I still would not care. I already have CS2 and Apex Legends for when I want to sweat my balls off. And even if I didn't, I would still have games like Fortnite, Valorant and Siege. And if you absolutely want an extraction shooter, you have Tarkov. What I need is more casual games that I can play with friends or alone. And there is NO such thing as a casual PvP shooter experience. People will min-max this game within weeks. I want more studios to create games like Hazelight makes. Games that can only be beaten with 2 players, making the entire experience a shared effort, while also having it be casual and wholesome. When I am in the mood for sweating and trolling, I have soooo many options. The market feels saturated with such games. Meanwhile the Coop scene has a massive vacuum that is looking to be filled. Not really related to any of this mess, but that has been my general sentiment for a couple of years now. Any time a new PvP shooter comes out all I can think is "I do not care about this game at all."
